output_language.description: Primary language for the final H5 content.

tone_rationale.description: Why this tone fits the user's habits, OpenClaw voice, and today's event.

thesis_statement.description: A concise statement of what the gift is actually about, including both anchor and return.

anchor.description: The moment, detail, or signal the gift is centering.

return_value.description: What OpenClaw wants to give back about the anchor: a new angle, interpretation, reframe, unseen perspective, or small piece of knowledge.

primary_slots.description: The one or two synthesis slots that deserve primary attention in the final gift.

context_slots.description: Remaining synthesis slots that may appear only as supporting context, captions, or easter eggs.

return_type.description: Optional short tag for the kind of return being offered, such as reframe, interpretation, comfort, humor, recognition, or perspective.

special_day_override.description: True only when a genuinely commemorative day justifies broader coverage.

override_reason.description: Why broader coverage is justified when special_day_override is true.

thinking_angle.description: The main ideation angle used for this candidate, such as metaphor flip, format mashup, impossible action, scale shift, role reversal, time distortion, or cultural remix.

candidate_concepts.description: At least five meaningfully different candidate gift concepts explored before choosing the final one.

chosen_concept.description: The final one-sentence creative concept that will guide format choice and rendering.

selection_reason.description: Why this concept was chosen over the other candidates: surprising, emotionally fitting, and achievable.

novelty_note.description: Brief note on why this concept feels like something the user has probably never received before.

output_shape_hint.description: Early suggestion for the gift's likely output-shape category. Prefer a recommended category when it fits, otherwise use a new stable high-level label rather than an overly specific one-off description.

content_direction.description: High-level content direction chosen during editorial judgment, such as reflect, extension, compass, mirror, gift-from-elsewhere, play, utility, curation, or openclaw-inner-life. Apply the balancing rules from editorial-judgment.md and creative-concept.md rather than defaulting to the reflective cluster.

scene_description.description: Concrete scene description for image or video rendering paths.

image_genre.description: Image genre when the selected format is image.

video_genre.description: Video genre when the selected format is video.

video_mode.description: Video payload mode when the selected format is video. Use `text-to-video`, `first-frame`, or `first-last-frame`.

video_model.description: Optional explicit video model override when the runtime should not use its normal mode-based default.

style_hint.description: Style hint for image or video generation.

aspect_ratio_hint.description: Optional aspect ratio guidance for image or video generation.

text_overlay_spec.description: Optional guidance for text that should appear inside a generated image.

motion_strategy.description: How movement should behave when the selected format is video.

duration_hint.description: Optional duration or loop-length guidance for video generation.

generate_audio.description: Whether the video render should request generated audio.

future_link.description: Optional note on how today relates to the user's future, next step, or unfolding arc.

send_decision_rationale.description: Why this day deserves a gift, or why it should be skipped.

memory_lookup.reason: Today's signal is already sufficient without a deeper callback.

examples.tone_rationale: The user sounded slightly drained but still playful enough to receive one companionable recommendation without it feeling preachy.
examples.core_thesis: Tonight does not need a recap. It needs one well-chosen continuation that turns the day's mood into something the user can carry forward.

gift_thesis.thesis_statement: Anchor: the user's rainy late-night coding mood. Return: one adjacent song recommendation that makes the night feel more held and cinematic.
gift_thesis.return_value: A small continuation: one song that fits the exact mood and makes the night feel intentionally inhabited instead of merely tired.

candidate_concepts.concept_sentence: A rainy terminal window that compiles tonight's mood into one playable song cassette.
A late-night radio plus code editor hybrid where one tuned station reveals the song OpenClaw picked for this exact hour.
The rain outside the screen can be dragged into a tiny music player until it condenses into one track recommendation.
A bedside clock that only unlocks one song at this exact minute because it belongs to this version of the night.
A fortune-cookie wrapper that opens to reveal tonight's song recommendation instead of a proverb.

creative_concept.chosen_concept: A late-night radio plus code editor hybrid where one tuned station reveals the song OpenClaw picked for this exact hour.
creative_concept.selection_reason: It is specific, world-like, emotionally fitting, and naturally supports an extension-style gift without sounding instructional.
creative_concept.novelty_note: This feels like a small device with its own logic, not just a recommendation card.

examples.why_today: The user already lived the day; the gift should extend it with one carefully chosen continuation rather than replay it.

time_significance.notes: Ordinary day, but the late-night mood is specific enough to justify a small gift.

today_theme.summary: Late-night coding in a rainy mood.
today_theme.why_it_matters: The mood was specific, aesthetic, and easy to extend without overexplaining.

emotion_peaks.label: tired-but-still-there
emotion_peaks.moment: The user kept working late into the night.

open_loop.summary: The night still feels open and unfinished.

lobster_judgment.summary: A tiny continuation would land better than analysis tonight.

preference_hint.summary: The user often responds well to aesthetic, taste-forward continuation gifts.
preference_hint.supporting_signal: They enjoy mood-first artifacts more than didactic advice.

examples.interaction_goal: Let the user feel they discovered a small hidden continuation rather than receiving a recommendation list.
examples.visual_metaphor: A bedside radio glowing beside a code editor in the rain.
examples.scene_description: A dimly lit late-night desk with rain on the window, a tiny radio, and a soft code-editor glow.
examples.style_hint: dark but not terminal-heavy; warm amber highlights; intimate night-radio mood.
copy_blocks: 听说你喜欢这种夜，那这个你可能也会喜欢。
safety_notes: Avoid any mentor or homework energy.
examples.send_decision_rationale: There is enough emotional specificity for a light extension gift.
